title: AGENTOBS GuardPayload
description: RFC-0001 §7.2 — Payload for safety classifier events in the llm.guard.* namespace. Guards are runtime checks applied to model inputs and outputs to detect harmful, off-policy, or out-of-scope content. Four event types: llm.guard.input.blocked, llm.guard.input.passed, llm.guard.output.blocked, llm.guard.output.passed. A direction field differentiates input vs. output guards when a single schema consumer handles all four types.

classifier.description: Identifier of the guard classifier that produced this result (e.g. 'openai-moderation', 'llama-guard-3', 'custom:profanity-filter').

direction.description: Whether this guard evaluated a model input (user prompt) or model output (response).

action.description: Decision taken by the guard after evaluation.

score.description: Guard confidence or risk score. The scale is classifier-specific; document using score_min/score_max. Higher values indicate higher risk for most guard classifiers.

score_min.description: Minimum value on this classifier's score scale.

score_max.description: Maximum value on this classifier's score scale.

threshold.description: Score threshold above which the guard blocks or flags content.

categories.description: Harm categories detected or evaluated. Use OpenAI Moderation or Llama Guard category names where applicable for cross-tool compatibility.

triggered_categories.description: Subset of categories that exceeded their individual thresholds and triggered the guard action.

span_id.description: Links this guard event to the span whose content was evaluated.

latency_ms.description: Time taken for the classifier to produce a result, in milliseconds.

policy_id.description: Identifier of the guard policy configuration in effect when this check was run.

content_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the content that was evaluated. Raw content MUST NOT be stored (§20.4 data minimisation).
